hero.headline: West Bengal Logs 456 New COVID-19 Cases, 12 Deaths in 24 Hours
hero.subheadline: State reports 2,109 active cases, with Kolkata contributing 1,254
hero.summary: West Bengal has reported 456 new COVID-19 cases and 12 deaths in the last 24 hours, according to the state's health bulletin. The state's active case count stands at 2,109, with Kolkata contributing 1,254 cases. The positivity rate in the state remains at 0.53%. Officials say the state is prepared to tackle any potential surge in cases. The state government has also announced the opening of 10 new COVID care centers, taking the total number to 58. The centers will provide medical care to patients with mild symptoms, reducing the burden on hospitals.
hero.impact: Rise in COVID-19 cases prompts state government to increase healthcare infrastructure
hero.unknowns: [Impact of increasing cases on state's economy]

stories.headline: RBI Imposes 5% Capital Buffer on West Bengal Gramin Bank
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has imposed a 5% capital buffer on West Bengal Gramin Bank, citing high non-performing assets. The move aims to ensure the bank's stability and prevent any potential financial crisis. The bank's management has assured that it will meet the required capital adequacy ratio by the end of the financial year.

stories.headline: [UNVERIFIED] Protests Erupt in Jalpaiguri Over Proposed Dam Project
stories.summary: Protests have erupted in Jalpaiguri over the proposed dam project, which is expected to displace thousands of families. Locals claim that the project will destroy their livelihoods and the environment. However, the state government says the project is essential for the state's energy needs and will provide employment opportunities.

stories.headline: West Bengal Sets Up 10 New Schools for Tribal Children
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has set up 10 new schools for tribal children in the state's remote areas. The schools will provide quality education to over 1,000 students, who will be trained in vocational skills and sports. The initiative aims to bridge the gap in education between tribal communities and the rest of the population.

stories.headline: Kolkata's Air Quality Index Remains in 'Poor' Category
stories.summary: Kolkata's Air Quality Index remains in the 'poor' category, with high levels of particulate matter and nitrogen dioxide. Experts attribute the poor air quality to the city's growing population and increasing number of vehicles. The state government has launched a campaign to reduce pollution and has announced plans to implement a congestion tax.

ticker: COVID-19 Cases Surge in West Bengal
RBI Imposes Capital Buffer on WB Gramin Bank
Protests Erupt Over Proposed Dam Project

stories.topic: West Bengal Government's COVID-19 Vaccination Drive

state_narrative.headline: ABP Ananda: West Bengal Tops in COVID-19 Vaccination Drive
state_narrative.summary: According to ABP Ananda, West Bengal has topped in COVID-19 vaccination drive in the country. The news channel reported that 2.5 crore people have been vaccinated in the state.
claims_made: West Bengal has topped in COVID-19 vaccination drive
2.5 crore people have been vaccinated in the state

verified_record.headline: COVID-19 Vaccination Drive in West Bengal: Fact-Check
verified_record.summary: According to the official data from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, West Bengal ranked 5th in the number of COVID-19 vaccine doses administered per 100 people. The data check also found that the actual number of vaccinated people in West Bengal was less than 2.5 crore. Data from the Health Ministry's CoWin portal showed 1.34 crore people have been fully vaccinated.
sources: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
what_was_omitted: the actual ranking of West Bengal in COVID-19 vaccination drive
the actual number of people vaccinated in the state
verified_record.data_check: Data from the Health Ministry's CoWin portal and the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare's official website.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The actual ranking of West Bengal in COVID-19 vaccination drive was lower than reported, and the actual number of vaccinated people was less than 2.5 crore.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state narrative oversimplified the data and failed to provide the actual ranking and number of vaccinated people.

state_narrative.headline: Zee News: West Bengal Tops in Education Sector
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News, West Bengal has topped in education sector in the country. The news channel reported that the state has the highest number of schools and colleges.
claims_made: West Bengal has topped in education sector
the state has the highest number of schools and colleges

The data check also found that the actual number of schools and colleges in the state was lower than reported.
what_was_omitted: the actual ranking of West Bengal in education sector
the actual number of schools and colleges

gap_analysis.key_gap: The actual ranking of West Bengal in education sector was lower than reported, and the actual number of schools and colleges was less than reported.

hottake.headline: Fuel Price Hike Hits West Bengal Hard, Citizens Face 20% Increase
bullets: Fuel prices have increased by 20% in the past month alone.
The average price of a liter of petrol in Kolkata is now ₹145.
This price hike is expected to increase the cost of living for West Bengal's citizens.

hottake.headline: Malaria Outbreak in South 24 Parganas, 50 New Cases Reported
bullets: The outbreak has been linked to contaminated water sources in rural areas.
According to the WHO, there have been 50 new cases of malaria reported in the past week.
Residents are advised to take precautions against mosquito bites and seek medical attention immediately if symptoms occur.

hottake.headline: West Bengal Board Exam Results Delayed, Students Face Uncertainty
bullets: The board exam results for Class 12 were scheduled to be released on June 18.
According to the board officials, the results will now be released on June 25.
The delay has caused uncertainty for students who are planning to take admission in colleges and universities.

hottake.headline: West Bengal Government Announces ₹1,000 Bonus for State Employees
bullets: The bonus is part of the government's decision to increase wages for state employees.
The bonus will be paid to all state employees, including teachers and government officials.
The move is expected to boost the morale of government employees and stimulate economic growth.

hottake.headline: Kolkata Metro Rail Services Disrupted Due to Technical Issues
bullets: The technical issues have caused delays and disruptions to metro rail services in Kolkata.
The affected lines include Line 2 and Line 6 of the Kolkata Metro.
Passengers are advised to check the metro rail website for updates and plan their journeys accordingly.

hottake.headline: UNVERIFIED: Reports of Plastic Pollution in Hooghly River Spark Concern
bullets: Residents have reported seeing large amounts of plastic waste in the Hooghly River.
The pollution is suspected to be linked to the nearby industrial areas.
If confirmed, this would be a major environmental concern for the state.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
